attributes.content:
## Overview

The Knowledge Graph Node card gives the workspace a structured home for a node in a knowledge graph (Roam Research-style). Bidirectional links, backlinks, and graph visualization. Visualizes how things relate to each other.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Knowledge Graph Node usually evolves through drafting, review, and reuse. The card keeps its core content structured enough to be surfaced in different views, linked from related records, and updated by people or automation without losing provenance.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Knowledge Graph Node can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Note]]** -- Note can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Knowledge Graph Node.
- **[[Article]]** -- Article can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Knowledge Graph Node.
- **[[Prompt Template]]** -- Prompt-driven drafting or summarization can turn Knowledge Graph Node into a reusable AI-assisted workflow.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Knowledge Graph Node can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Project]]** -- Knowledge Graph Node can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Knowledge Graph Node as the structured home for a node in a knowledge graph (Roam Research-style). Bidirectional links, backlinks, and graph visualization. Visualizes how things relate to each other and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Knowledge Graph Node when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Knowledge Graph Node stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
